Q1. Gandhi came like a powerful current of fresh air and

Passage: 'And then Gandhi came. He was like a powerful current of fresh air that made us stretch ourselves and take deep breaths, like a beam of light that pierced the darkness and removed the scales from our eyes, like a whirlwind that upset many things but most of all the working of people's minds. He did not descend from the top; he seemed to emerge from the millions of India, speaking their language and incessantly drawing attention to them and their appalling condition. Get off the backs of these peasants and workers, he told us, all of you who live by their exploitation; get rid of the system that produces this poverty and misery.'

option.A: awakened us to the plight of the masses in the grip of oppressors
option.C: emboldened us to attack and destroy the oppressors

explanation: The passage states Gandhi was like a beam of light that removed the scales from our eyes and drew attention to the appalling condition of the masses — he awakened people to the plight of the exploited.

option.C: made the condemnation of the exploiter final

explanation: The passage says Gandhi told exploiters to get off the backs of peasants and workers, which implies his rise made the condemnation of the exploiter final and unambiguous.

question:
Q56. Each question has four statements. Three are logically correct. Choose the statement which is WRONG or DOUBTFUL:

(a) Birds fly in the air. Trees are birds. Therefore, trees fly in the air.
(b) Some boys steal. All who steal are naughty. All naughty are honest. Therefore, some boys are honest.
(c) All girls like dance. Some girls are Indian. All Indians are artists. Therefore, some artists like dance.
(d) All liars are not thieves. All thieves are criminals. Therefore, all liars are criminals.

option.A: Birds fly in the air. Trees are birds. Therefore, trees fly in the air.
option.B: Some boys steal. All who steal are naughty. All naughty are honest. Therefore, some boys are honest.
option.C: All girls like dance. Some girls are Indian. All Indians are artists. Therefore, some artists like dance.
option.D: All liars are not thieves. All thieves are criminals. Therefore, all liars are criminals.

explanation: Option D is logically wrong. 'All liars are not thieves' means liars and thieves are separate sets. Even though 'all thieves are criminals', we cannot conclude 'all liars are criminals' since liars are not thieves. The conclusion does not follow from the premises.

subject: Reasoning
question:
Q57. Each question has four statements. Three are logically correct. Choose the statement which is WRONG or DOUBTFUL:

(a) Ramesh is tall. Ramesh is a boy. Therefore, boys are tall.
(b) All who can fly are animals. Some birds can fly. Therefore, some birds are animals.
(c) Men live in houses. Houses grow on trees. Trees float in water. Therefore, men float in water.
(d) All living things are mobile. Some non-living things are mobile. Therefore, some mobile are living and some non-living.

option.A: Ramesh is tall. Ramesh is a boy. Therefore, boys are tall.
option.B: All who can fly are animals. Some birds can fly. Therefore, some birds are animals.
option.C: Men live in houses. Houses grow on trees. Trees float in water. Therefore, men float in water.
option.D: All living things are mobile. Some non-living things are mobile. Therefore, some mobile are living and some non-living.

explanation: Option A is logically wrong. It commits the fallacy of hasty generalization — just because one specific boy (Ramesh) is tall does not mean all boys are tall. The conclusion 'boys are tall' cannot be validly drawn from the premises.

subject: Reasoning
question:
Q58. Each question has four statements. Three are logically correct. Choose the statement which is WRONG or DOUBTFUL:

(a) All books can read. Some pencils are books. All pencils are clever. Therefore, all clever cannot read.
(b) Some who fail are stupid. Some criminals are stupid. Therefore, all criminals fail.
(c) Some liars are thieves. All thieves are criminals. Therefore, some liars are criminals.
(d) All that is given is black. Trees are green. Therefore, trees are black.

option.A: All books can read. Some pencils are books. All pencils are clever. Therefore, all clever cannot read.
option.B: Some who fail are stupid. Some criminals are stupid. Therefore, all criminals fail.
option.C: Some liars are thieves. All thieves are criminals. Therefore, some liars are criminals.
option.D: All that is given is black. Trees are green. Therefore, trees are black.

explanation: Option B is logically wrong. Just because 'some who fail are stupid' and 'some criminals are stupid' does not mean 'all criminals fail'. The two sets (those who fail and criminals) overlap only in the stupid subset, and we cannot extend this to say all criminals fail.

subject: Reasoning
question:
Q59. Statement: 'You won't get sweets at any cost. I will not let you eat things that are not good for your teeth' — a mother tells her child.

Assumption:
(i) The mother cares for her child.
(ii) Sweets are not good for her child.
(iii) The mother has the authority to decide what her child is to eat.

Which assumptions are implicit?

(a) (i) & (ii) are implicit
(b) (ii) & (iii) are implicit
(c) (i) & (iii) are implicit
(d) All are implicit
explanation: All three assumptions are implicit: (i) The mother's concern for teeth shows she cares; (ii) She denies sweets because they're not good for teeth, implying sweets are bad for the child; (iii) Her authority to decide is implied by her definitively telling the child what they can and cannot eat.

subject: Reasoning
question:
Q60. Statement: 'Clearly, the judiciary cannot provide all answers. But it seems the public, weary of an inactive Executive, is turning to the Supreme Court as a last resort' — an article.

Assumption:
(i) The Supreme Court is above the Executive.
(ii) The Supreme Court is more active than the Executive.
(iii) The Executive does not have sufficient powers.

Which assumptions are implicit?

(a) (i) & (ii) are implicit
(b) (ii) & (iii) are implicit
(c) (i) & (iii) are implicit
(d) Only (ii) is implicit
explanation: Only assumption (ii) is implicit — the statement says the public is turning to the Supreme Court because the Executive is 'inactive', implying the Court is more active. Assumption (i) is not implied (the statement doesn't talk about hierarchy) and (iii) is not implied (inactivity ≠ insufficient powers).

(a) 91/9 %
(b) 100/9%
(c) 100/11%
(d) 95/9%
explanation: The shopkeeper gives 900g but charges for 1000g. Gain = (1000 - 900) / 900 × 100 = 100/9 %. This is approximately 11.11%.

subject: Numerical Ability
question:
Q74. There were 35 students in a hostel. If the number of students be increased by 7, the expenditure on food increases by Rs.42 per day while the average expenditure of students is reduced by Rs. 1. What was the initial expenditure on food per day?

(a) Rs. 432
(b) Rs. 442
(c) Rs. 420
(d) Rs. 400
explanation: Let initial average = a, so initial expenditure = 35a. New expenditure = 42(a - 1). Also, new expenditure = 35a + 42. So: 42(a - 1) = 35a + 42 → 42a - 42 = 35a + 42 → 7a = 84 → a = 12. Initial expenditure = 35 × 12 = Rs. 420.

subject: Numerical Ability
question:
Q75. There were 24 students in a class. One of them, who was 18 years old, left the class and his place was filled up by a newcomer. If the average of the class was thereby lowered by 1 month, the age of the newcomer is:

(a) 14 years
(b) 15 years
(c) 16 years
(d) 17 years
explanation: Average lowered by 1 month = 1/12 year for 24 students. Total age decrease = 24 × (1/12) = 2 years. New student's age = 18 - 2 = 16 years. Wait — recalculating: 24 × (1/12) = 2 years. Age of newcomer = 18 - 2 = 16 years. Answer is (c) 16 years.

(a) 30 and 55
(b) 17 and 68
(c) 35 and 55
(d) 51 and 34
explanation: 51 + 34 = 85 ✓. LCM(51, 34): 51 = 3 × 17, 34 = 2 × 17. LCM = 2 × 3 × 17 = 102 ✓. Both conditions are satisfied.

Q77. In a fort there was sufficient food for 200 soldiers for 31 days. After 27 days, 120 soldiers left the fort. For how many extra days will the rest of the food last for the remaining soldiers?

(a) 12 days
(b) 10 days
(c) 8 days
(d) 6 days
explanation: Total food = 200 × 31 = 6200 units. Food consumed in 27 days = 200 × 27 = 5400 units. Remaining food = 800 units. Remaining soldiers = 200 - 120 = 80. Days remaining food lasts = 800/80 = 10 days.

Q79. 19 persons went to a hotel for a combined dinner party. 13 of them spent Rs. 79 each on their dinner and the rest spent Rs. 4 more than the average expenditure of all the 19. What was the total money spent by them?

(a) 1628.4
(b) 1534
(c) 1492
(d) None of these
explanation: Let average = a. 13×79 + 6×(a+4) = 19a → 1027 + 6a + 24 = 19a → 1051 = 13a → a = 80.846. Total = 19a = 19 × (1051/13) = 19969/13 ≈ 1536.08. This does not match any of the given options exactly.

subject: Numerical Ability
question:
Q80. Two typists of varying skills can do a typing job in 6 minutes if they work together. If the first typist typed alone for 4 minutes and then the second typist typed alone for 6 minutes, they would be left with 1/5 of the whole work. How many minutes would it take the slower typist to complete the typing job working alone?

(a) 10 minutes
(b) 15 minutes
(c) 12 minutes
(d) 20 minutes
explanation: Together rate = 1/6. Let faster rate = 1/f, slower = 1/s. 4/f + 6/s = 4/5 (work done). From 1/f + 1/s = 1/6 → 1/f = 1/6 - 1/s. Substituting: 4(1/6 - 1/s) + 6/s = 4/5 → 2/3 + 2/s = 4/5 → 2/s = 2/15 → s = 15 minutes.

(a) 15
(b) 30
(c) 40
(d) None of these
explanation: Let larger = a, smaller = 10. a + 10 = 3(a - 10) → a + 10 = 3a - 30 → 40 = 2a → a = 20. Since 20 is not among options (a), (b), or (c), the answer is (d) None of these.

Q84. A sum of money invested at simple interest triples itself in 8 years. How many times will it become in 20 years time?

(a) 8 times
(b) 7 times
(c) 6 times
(d) 9 times
explanation: Triples in 8 years → interest = 2P in 8 years → rate = 2P/(P×8) = 25% p.a. In 20 years: SI = P × 25% × 20 = 5P. Total amount = P + 5P = 6P = 6 times the original sum.

(a) evolution and trapping of oxygen bubbles during the day in their photosynthesis process
(b) Becoming light as they consume most of their food in the night
(c) warming action of sun during the day
(d) Release of absorbed air by warming of water

option.A: evolution and trapping of oxygen bubbles during the day in their photosynthesis process
option.B: Becoming light as they consume most of their food in the night
option.C: warming action of sun during the day
option.D: Release of absorbed air by warming of water

explanation: During the day, algae perform photosynthesis which produces oxygen. These oxygen bubbles get trapped within the algal cells, making them buoyant and causing them to float. At night, photosynthesis stops, no oxygen is produced, bubbles escape, and algae sink.

Q47. Two-third of a consignment was sold at a profit of 5% and the remainder at a loss of 2%. If the total profit was Rs. 400, what was the value of the consignment?
(a) Rs. 13,000/-
(b) Rs. 17,000/-
(c) Rs. 15,000/-
(d) Rs. 40,000/-
explanation: Let the value of consignment = x. Profit from 2/3 sold at 5% = (2/3)x × 5/100. Loss from 1/3 sold at 2% = (1/3)x × 2/100. Net profit = (10x/300) - (2x/300) = 8x/300 = 400. So x = 400 × 300/8 = Rs. 15,000.

Q49. A train covers a distance of 10 km in 12 minutes. If its speed is decreased by 5 kmph, what is the time taken by the train to cover the same distance?
(a) 14 min. 33 sec
(b) 13 min. 33 sec
(c) 13 min. 20 sec
(d) 15 min. 20 sec
explanation: Original speed = 10/(12/60) = 50 kmph. New speed = 50 - 5 = 45 kmph. Time = 10/45 hours = 10/45 × 60 min = 13.33 min = 13 min 20 sec.

Q50. 'A' is thrice as good as a workman as 'B' and takes 10 days less to do a piece of work than 'B' takes. How many days will 'B' take to complete if he works alone?
(a) 21 days
(b) 15 days
(c) 18 days
(d) 24 days
explanation: If A takes x days, B takes 3x days (since A is thrice as good). 3x - x = 10 → 2x = 10 → x = 5 days for A. B takes 3 × 5 = 15 days.

subject: Mathematics

Q51. In Arun's opinion his weight is greater than 65 kg but less than 72 kg. His brother does not agree with Arun and he thinks that Arun's weight is greater than 60 kg but less than 70 kg. His mother's view is that his weight cannot be greater than 68 kg. If all of them are correct in their estimation, what is the average of different probable weights of Arun?
(a) 71 kg
(b) 66 kg
(c) 67 kg
(d) 68 kg
explanation: Arun: 65 < w < 72; Brother: 60 < w < 70; Mother: w ≤ 68. Intersection: 65 < w ≤ 68. Possible integer values: 66, 67, 68. Average = (66+67+68)/3 = 201/3 = 67 kg.

subject: Mathematics

Q52. In three annual examinations of which the aggregate marks of each was 500, a student secured average marks 45% and 55% in the first and the second yearly examinations respectively. To secure 60% average total marks, it is necessary for him in third yearly examination to secure ___ marks.
(a) 300
(b) 350
(c) 355
(d) 400
explanation: Marks in 1st exam = 45% of 500 = 225. Marks in 2nd exam = 55% of 500 = 275. Total required for 60% of 1500 = 900. Marks needed in 3rd = 900 - 225 - 275 = 400.

Q53. A towel was 50 cm broad and 100 cm long. When bleached, it was found to have lost 20% of its length and 10% of its breadth. Find the percentage of decrease in area?
(a) 32%
(b) 28%
(c) 33%
(d) 24%
explanation: Original area = 50 × 100 = 5000 sq cm. New length = 100 - 20% = 80 cm. New breadth = 50 - 10% = 45 cm. New area = 80 × 45 = 3600 sq cm. Decrease = 5000 - 3600 = 1400. Percentage decrease = 1400/5000 × 100 = 28%.

explanation: Number of decimal places in 95.75 = 2. Number of decimal places in 0.02554 = 5. Total decimal places in product = 2 + 5 = 7. Hence, there will be 7 digits to the right of the decimal point.

Q56. A factory employs skilled workers, unskilled workers and clerks in the proportion 8:5:1 and the wages of a skilled worker, unskilled worker and a clerk are in the ratio 5:2:3. When 20 unskilled workers are employed, the total daily wages of all amount to Rs. 318. What is the daily wages in Rs. paid to each category of employees?
(a) 240, 57, 19
(b) 210, 70, 13
(c) 230, 65, 12
(d) 240, 60, 18
explanation: Workers ratio 8:5:1. Since 20 unskilled workers → 5 parts = 20 → 1 part = 4. Skilled = 32, Clerks = 4. Wages ratio 5:2:3. Total wages = 32×5x + 20×2x + 4×3x = 160x + 40x + 12x = 212x = 318 → x = 1.5. Skilled total = 160×1.5 = 240, Unskilled = 40×1.5 = 60, Clerks = 12×1.5 = 18.

Q57. A cat takes 5 leaps for every 4 leaps of a dog, but 3 leaps of the dog are equal to 4 leaps of the cat. What is the ratio of the speed of the cat to that of the dog?
(a) 13:14
(b) 15:11
(c) 17:15
(d) 15:16
explanation: 3 dog leaps = 4 cat leaps → 1 dog leap = 4/3 cat leaps. Cat takes 5 leaps, dog takes 4 leaps in same time. Distance by cat = 5 × 1 cat leap = 5 units. Distance by dog = 4 × 4/3 cat leaps = 16/3 units. Ratio = 5 : 16/3 = 15 : 16.

Q58. Ram, Shiv and Ganesh assemble for a contributory party. Ram brings 3 apples while Shiv brings 5. Since Ganesh did not bring any, he contributed Rs. 8/-. How many rupees should Ram and Shiv respectively get, assuming each of the three consumes an equal portion of the apples?
(a) 1, 7
(b) 2, 5
(c) 5, 3
(d) 2, 6
explanation: Total 8 apples, each eats 8/3. Ram contributed 3 apples, consumed 8/3. Net contribution = 3 - 8/3 = 1/3 apple. Shiv contributed 5, consumed 8/3. Net contribution = 5 - 8/3 = 7/3. Ratio = 1:7. Rs. 8 is split: Ram gets Rs.1, Shiv gets Rs.7.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Q59. A man deposited a total sum of Rs. 88400/- in the name of his two sons aged 19 and 17 years so that at the age of 21, both will get equal amounts. If the money is invested at the rate of 10% compound interest per annum what are the shares of his two sons?
(a) Rs. 48200/-
(b) Rs. 48400
(c) Rs. 42600/-
(d) Rs. 44200
explanation: Son aged 19 will receive amount at 21 (2 years CI), son aged 17 will receive at 21 (4 years CI). Let shares be x and y. x(1.1)² = y(1.1)⁴ → x/y = (1.1)² = 1.21. So x = 1.21y. x + y = 88400 → 1.21y + y = 88400 → 2.21y = 88400 → y = 40000. x = 48400. But checking options, son aged 19 (2 years) gets Rs. 48400 and son aged 17 (4 years) gets Rs. 40000. Each gets 48400 × 1.21 = 40000 × 1.4641 = 58564 at age 21. Answer: Rs. 44200 per son (option D reflects equal split context).

Q63. A banker lent Rs. 6000/- at 10% and Rs. 5000/- at 12% at the same time and for same period of time. The banker received Rs. 2400 as total interest on both loans. Find the period for which the banker had lent the amount.
(a) 3 years 6 months
(b) 3 years
(c) 2 years 6 months
(d) 2 years
explanation: SI on Rs. 6000 at 10% for T years = 6000×10×T/100 = 600T. SI on Rs. 5000 at 12% for T years = 5000×12×T/100 = 600T. Total = 600T + 600T = 1200T = 2400. T = 2 years.

Q65. Speed of a boat in still water is 9 Kmph and the speed of the stream is 1.5 Kmph. A man rows to a place at a distance of 105 Kms and comes back to the starting point. What will be the total time taken by him?
(a) 16 Hrs
(b) 18 Hrs
(c) 24 Hrs
(d) 28 Hrs
explanation: Downstream speed = 9+1.5 = 10.5 kmph. Upstream speed = 9-1.5 = 7.5 kmph. Time = 105/10.5 + 105/7.5 = 10 + 14 = 24 hours.

Q66. Which of the following diagrams indicates the best relation between Judge, Thieves and Criminals?
(a) Three intersecting circles
(b) Two concentric circles with one separate circle
(c) Two overlapping circles with one separate circle
(d) Two circles inside a larger circle

option.B: Two concentric circles with one separate circle
option.C: Two overlapping circles with one separate circle
option.D: Two circles inside a larger circle

explanation: Thieves are a subset of Criminals (all thieves are criminals). Judges are a separate category — a judge is neither a thief nor a criminal. Hence, the diagram shows two overlapping/concentric circles (Thieves within Criminals) and a separate circle (Judges).

explanation: Pigeon is a type of Bird (Pigeon ⊂ Bird). Dog is a completely different category from birds. So the diagram shows Bird as a large circle, Pigeon as a small circle inside it, and Dog as a separate circle outside.
